name: Pina Colada Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
2. Line a 10-inch glass pie dish with foil, overlapping sides. Finely chop the first three ingredients in a food processor.
3. Add butter and blend until moist crumbs form. Press mixture onto the bottom and up the sides (but not the rim) of the prepared pie dish. Freeze for 10 minutes.
4. Bake the crust until golden brown, about 15 minutes. Then freeze for an additional 20 minutes.
5. Spread the softened pineapple sorbet in the cooled crust, smoothing the top.
6. Boil the cream of Coconut in a small saucepan over medium-high heat until reduced to 3/4 cup, stirring frequently, about 7 minutes.
7. Pour into a large bowl and mix in rum and extract, then another 3/4 cup coconut. Let it cool slightly.
8. Add frozen yogurt to the coconut mixture and fold until blended.
9. Freeze until semifirm, stirring occasionally, about 1 hour.
10. Spoon the filling over the sorbet in the pie dish, mounding slightly. Cover with foil.
11. Freeze overnight (or up to 2 weeks ahead). To serve, turn out the pie onto a platter, peel off the foil, and turn it right side up.
12. Arrange pineapple slices decoratively atop. Sprinkle with toasted coconut.
13. Garnish with mint leaves. Enjoy!
